tldr: These tools serve different purposes: MIMIC Replay is a self-hosted rrweb-based session replay framework, while Maze is a hosted user research and prototype testing platform. A direct migration is not realistic; this is a tool change for a different workflow.
why_switch: Maze is turnkey, no infra to run
Native prototype, card sort and tree testing
Built for design and research teams
Recordings tied to research tasks, not raw traffic
Faster path to UX insights than self-hosted rrweb
what_to_export: Any internal dashboards or scripts built on top
export_method: Because MIMIC Replay is self-hosted, you control the storage. Identify where rrweb event payloads and network interceptor data are persisted (object storage, database or filesystem) and snapshot the directories or tables you care about. Document the retention rules in place. Note that rrweb event JSON is only meaningful inside an rrweb-compatible player, so archived sessions effectively become cold artifacts unless you keep a player available.
import_method: Maze does not import production session recordings. Instead, set up Maze studies that match the questions you used MIMIC for: prototype usability tests, card sorts, tree tests, surveys. Connect Figma or the URL of your live app, define tasks, recruit participants from your audience or Maze's panel and run unmoderated tests. The output is task-scoped recordings and quantitative metrics inside Maze, not a continuation of historical rrweb data. Plan to keep MIMIC running in archive mode for old recordings if you need to refer back.

concept_mapping.target: Recruited participants in a Maze study

effort: 2-4 weeks to redesign workflow around research studies
gotchas: Maze is not a production session replay tool; you cannot stream live user sessions into it.
Historical rrweb recordings cannot be imported into Maze.
Engineering-focused error triage on live users disappears with this switch.
Maze higher-tier features may be required for advanced research methods.
Privacy posture changes: data leaves your infra and lands in Maze SaaS.

email.subject: Switching from MIMIC Replay to Maze in 14 days
email.body:
Hi team,

We are reorienting our UX work over the next 14 days. The team will move research and usability testing onto Maze and stop relying on our self-hosted MIMIC Replay deployment for that purpose.

During week one we will archive existing rrweb recordings and document what they covered. In week two we will stand up Maze studies that replace the questions MIMIC was helping us answer, then decommission the live MIMIC ingestion path.

If there are recordings you still need pulled, please flag them before the cutover so we can grab them from the self-hosted store.

Thanks,
Your customer
